# Salvage Priority: Top 10 Reusable Concepts

This document prioritizes the 10 highest-value, most-reusable assets and concepts from SmolbeanPlanet3D. Ranked by **implementation ROI** (value gained vs. adaptation effort).

---

## Priority Tier: MUST HAVE

### 1. Wave Function Collapse Solver + Schema

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5 — CRITICAL)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/WaveFunctionCollapse.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/WaveFunctionCollapse.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/NeighbourData.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/NeighbourData.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/NeighbourSelector.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/NeighbourSelector.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/Edge.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/Edge.cs)

**What You Get**:
- Proven constraint solver algorithm (tile adjacency validation)
- Adjacency rule format and precomputation pipeline
- Backtracking logic for handling dead-end collapses
- Integration with mesh boundary compatibility (NeighbourData)

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Decouple from MapData ScriptableObject
- **Medium**: Port to Burst job (rewrite loop + data structures)
- **High**: Add weighted entropy + deep backtracking (optional enhancements)

**Total Adaptation Time**: 2–3 weeks (Phase 1–2)

**Why Essential**:
- Directly solves your tile constraint problem
- Proven to work at 256×256 scale
- Core algorithm is portable and deterministic

**Next Step**: Extract to static utility class in Phase 1 ([migration-notes.md](migration-notes.md#12-wfc-constraint-solver-pure-utility))

---

### 2. Mesh Boundary Compatibility Authoring Tool

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5 — CRITICAL)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Editor/TerrainDataEditor.cs](../../Assets/Editor/TerrainDataEditor.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/TerrainData.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/TerrainData.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/MapData.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/MapData.cs)

**What You Get**:
- **Proprietary algorithm** for automatically deriving tile adjacency from mesh geometry
- Edge-matching logic (`BoundariesMatch()` in TerrainDataEditor)
- Pre-computation pipeline: Load mesh → analyze edges → store compatibility table
- UI for manual adjacency tweaking (inspector)

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Medium**: Rewrite mesh analysis for your mesh library (custom logic per project)
- **Low**: Constraint rule export format (use as-is)

**Total Adaptation Time**: 3–5 days (Phase 1 extraction)

**Why Essential**:
- Eliminates manual adjacency authoring (60+ constraint rules → auto-generated)
- Ensures mesh boundary pixel-perfect compatibility
- Creates ground truth for WFC solver

**Caution**: This algorithm is **specific to how SmolbeanPlanet3D edges are aligned**. You may need to customize for your own mesh convention.

**Next Step**: Port TerrainDataEditor logic to Phase 1 registry generation tool

---

### 3. Height Field Procedural Pipeline

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5 — FOUNDATIONAL)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs)

**What You Get**:
- Layered Perlin noise + island falloff curve
- Height remapping to target range (sea level → mountain peak)
- Mountain exaggeration (boost for dramatic peaks)
- Seeded for deterministic reproducibility

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Pure math; directly port to Burst
- **Medium**: Replace `Mathf.PerlinNoise` with seeded hash function (better cross-platform determinism)

**Total Adaptation Time**: 1 week (Phase 1)

**Why Essential**:
- Provides control input to WFC (height-based module filtering)
- Proven parameters for "interesting" terrain
- Handles falloff gracefully (prevents floating continents)

**Next Step**: Extract to static `HeightFieldGenerator` utility in Phase 1

---

## Priority Tier: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED

### 4. Terrain Mesh Asset Library + Metadata

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5 — HIGH VALUE)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Meshes/TerrainMeshesAutoGenerated/](../../Assets/Meshes/TerrainMeshesAutoGenerated/) (47 mesh files)
- [Assets/Settings/BuiltinAssets/TerrainData.asset](../../Assets/Settings/BuiltinAssets/TerrainData.asset)
- [Assets/Settings/BuiltinAssets/NeighbourData.asset](../../Assets/Settings/BuiltinAssets/NeighbourData.asset)

**What You Get**:
- **28 validated terrain mesh variants**:
  - Flat tiles (4 rotations = 4 variants)
  - Cliff faces (N/E/S/W = 4 variants)
  - Slopes (8 variants @ different angles)
  - Seabed → shallow water transitions
  - Terrain-to-waterfall edges
- **Pre-computed adjacency table** (NeighbourData.asset)
- **Material library** (terrain shaders, grass blending)

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Use meshes as-is OR update for your art style
- **Medium**: Regenerate adjacency table if you modify mesh library
- **High**: Add new mesh variants (requires re-running TerrainDataEditor)

**Total Adaptation Time**: 1–2 weeks (integration into Phase 1 registry)

**Why High Value**:
- **28 variants cover a surprising amount of spatial variety**
- Low-poly aesthetic matches target project
- Adjacency table is pre-validated (confidence + time savings)

**Caution**: Meshes are tailored to SmolbeanPlanet3D's specific scale (10 units/tile, certain height ranges). Verify compatibility with your coordinate system.

**Next Step**: Import meshes into Phase 1 registry; regenerate adjacency if needed

---

### 5. Terrain Wear + Feedback Texture System

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5 — NOVEL TECHNIQUE)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Terrain/GroundWearManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Terrain/GroundWearManager.cs)
- [Assets/Materials/GroundWithWearShader.shader](../../Assets/Materials/GroundWithWearShader.shader)
- [Assets/Scripts/UI/MapInteractionManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/UI/MapInteractionManager.cs) (wear application)

**What You Get**:
- **Texture-based wear tracking** (red channel encodes damage intensity)
- **Per-frame regrowth** (simulates healing/grass regrowth)
- **Efficient pixel manipulation** (`Texture2D.SetPixels32()` batching)
- **GPU-side blending** (wear integrates with terrain shader)
- **Circular/rectangular wear patterns** with optional blur

**Algorithm**:
```
1. Maintain RenderTexture of wear state (1 channel = damage intensity, 0–255)
2. On player action (stomp, farm, build):
   - Brush lower red channel in local region
   - Apply gradient blur (optional)
3. Per frame: Fade red channel upward (regrowth rate × deltaTime)
4. Shader blends wear texture with base terrain color
```

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Medium**: Extract from GroundWearManager singleton into utility
- **Medium-High**: Switch to RenderTexture for GPU-driven updates (or keep CPU texture updates if acceptable)
- **Low**: Shader is directly portable

**Total Adaptation Time**: 1–2 weeks (Phase 2, after ECS setup)

**Why High Value**:
- **Novel**: Most games use static terrain; this adds dynamic visual feedback
- **Efficient**: Texture approach scales better than per-quad damage tracking
- **Immersive**: Players see consequence of actions (footprints, paths, damage)

**Alternative Use**: Could adapt for mud, water, lava, or other transient ground effects.

**Next Step**: Benchmark cost of pixel updates at your target terrain size; consider compute shader port for scale

---

### 6. Instanced Grass Rendering + Vegetation Shading Patterns

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ (3/5 — GOOD IDEAS, NOT DOTS-READY AS-IS)

**Important Clarification**:
- The earlier 4/5-style assessment applies mostly to the **grass rendering and shading pattern**, not to the full vegetation runtime as a DOTS infinite-terrain solution.
- **Grass visual/render approach**: strong reuse candidate.
- **Tree runtime approach as implemented here**: weak for large streaming worlds.
- If separated, the rough score is:
  - **Grass rendering/shading**: 4/5
  - **Tree spawning/runtime model**: 2/5
  - **Combined vegetation system for DOTS infinite terrain**: 3/5

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/MapGeneration/GrassInstancer.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/MapGeneration/GrassInstancer.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/MapGeneration/GroundWearManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/MapGeneration/GroundWearManager.cs)
- [Assets/Materials/Nature/Grass/GrassMaterial.mat](../../Assets/Materials/Nature/Grass/GrassMaterial.mat)
- [Assets/Materials/Nature/WornGrass/GrassWithWearMaterial.mat](../../Assets/Materials/Nature/WornGrass/GrassWithWearMaterial.mat)
- [Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree4.prefab](../../Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree4.prefab)
- [Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree5.prefab](../../Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree5.prefab)
- [Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree6.prefab](../../Assets/Prefabs/Nature/Tree6.prefab)

**What You Actually Get**:
- **GPU-instanced grass rendering pattern** with batched draws and culling
- **Billboard/stylized grass shader approach** with wind and color variation
- **Wear-driven grass density/appearance feedback** tied to a world-space texture
- **A good visual recipe** for low-poly vegetation variation
- **A finite-world tree prefab setup** with 3 mesh variants, random scale/rotation, and shared stylized material

**What This Does NOT Give You**:
- A production-ready infinite-terrain vegetation runtime
- A chunk-streamed DOTS vegetation pipeline
- Scalable tree simulation at large world sizes
- A good far/mid/near tree LOD strategy

**Performance Reality**:
- **Grass draw cost** looks good in this project: about **1.74ms** after generation according to the notes in [GrassInstancer.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/MapGeneration/GrassInstancer.cs)
- **Grass generation cost** is the real weakness: multi-second setup in the same notes, plus CPU-side sampling/raycast filtering
- **Trees are not instanced here** in the same way grass is; they are full prefabs with collider, NavMeshObstacle, damage/fall/drop logic, which is fine near the player but not across an infinite streamed world

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Reuse the grass shading, wind, and wear ideas directly
- **Medium**: Port grass instancing to Entities Graphics or another chunked instancing path
- **High**: Replace tree runtime with chunk-based deterministic spawn records + near-player interactive promotion

**Total Adaptation Time**: 2–4 weeks depending on whether you keep only the visual ideas or rebuild the full vegetation pipeline for DOTS

**Why It Still Has Value**:
- **Grass visuals are good**: the project gets a lot of mileage from one instanced blade style plus transform/shader variation
- **Wear integration is genuinely useful**: this is a strong technique for a crafting/survival loop
- **Tree art direction works**: low-poly stylized trees with a few strong silhouettes can go far

**Cautions**:
- Do **not** read this section as "copy the vegetation system wholesale"
- The **rendering ideas are stronger than the spawning/runtime architecture**
- For your target project, the correct move is to keep the visual logic and replace the runtime model

**Recommended Target Architecture**:
1. Keep the grass instancing and shader ideas
2. Keep the wear-texture concept
3. Replace grass generation with chunk-local deterministic jobs
4. Replace full-world trees with render-only chunk trees plus near-player interactive tree promotion
5. Add LOD/HLOD/impostors for trees

**Next Step**: Treat this section as a vegetation art-and-render reference, not as a literal runtime blueprint; rebuild the spawning/runtime side for DOTS infinite terrain

---

## Priority Tier: USEFUL (Lower Effort, Good Gains)

### 7. Deterministic Seeded Generation Architecture

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ (3/5 — DESIGN PATTERN)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs) (Seed usage)
- [Assets/Scripts/Game/GameStateManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Game/GameStateManager.cs) (propagates seed)

**What You Get**:
- **Seed-driven reproducibility** across all generation stages
- **Xorshift PRNG** (deterministic across platforms/domain reloads)
- **Architectural pattern**: Inject seed into every random operation

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Create Xorshift128 utility struct
- **Low**: Plumb seed through your ECS systems

**Total Adaptation Time**: 2–3 days

**Why Useful**:
- **Multiplayer**: Same seed → same world on all clients (bandwidth savings)
- **Streaming**: Can regenerate chunks on-demand without re-transmitting
- **Replays**: Can replay game from seed + input log
- **Testing**: Deterministic maps enable regression testing

**Caution**: Global `Random` usage in SmolbeanPlanet3D breaks this. You must be disciplined.

**Next Step**: Phase 1 — Create Xorshift128 utility; audit all Random.Range calls

---

### 8. Secondary Feature Placement Pipeline (Trees, Rocks, Water)

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ (3/5 — ALGORITHM REUSE)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/TreeGenerator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/TreeGenerator.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/RockGenerator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/RockGenerator.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs) (water from height map)

**What You Get**:
- **Tree placement**: Noise-based probability + collision avoidance
- **Rock placement**: Similar noise-driven clustering
- **Water body detection**: Threshold-based from height map
- **Spatial hashing**: Fast collision checks for newborn objects

**Algorithm**:
```
For each grid cell:
  1. Sample Perlin noise → probability
  2. If random() < probability:
     a. Raycast down to find ground
     b. Check spatial hash (existing trees within radius)
     c. If clear → spawn prefab
```

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Low**: Algorithm is pure logic (no MonoBehaviour dependency)
- **Medium**: Port to Burst jobs (raycasts, spatial queries)

**Total Adaptation Time**: 1 week

**Why Useful**:
- **Procedural consistency**: Not random, but deterministic distribution
- **Avoids overlaps**: Collision detection prevents clipping
- **Scalable**: Burst-compiled jobs can handle many placement queries

**Next Step**: Extract to static utilities in Phase 1; port to jobs in Phase 2

---

### 9. Biome-Aware Module Weighting (Setup Foundation)

**Rank**: ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ (3/5 — FRAMEWORK, OPTIONAL IN PHASE 1)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/WaveFunctionCollapse.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/WaveFunctionCollapse.cs) (mentions biome)
- [Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Procedural/GameMapCreator.cs) (could compute biome map)

**What You Get**:
- **Conceptual framework** for biome-specific tile distributions
- **Example**: Tundra prefers snow/ice tiles; forest prefers grass/trees; desert prefers sand
- **Implementation hint**: Feed biome map to WFC as module weight modifier

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **Medium**: Compute biome map from climatic factors (temperature, moisture)
- **Medium**: Modify WFC to apply weight modifiers per biome

**Total Adaptation Time**: 1–2 weeks (Phase 3)

**Why Useful**:
- **Visual coherence**: Biomes cluster naturally; more immersive
- **Gameplay diversity**: Different regions have different affordances
- **Foundation for features**: Biome data feeds into flora/fauna spawning

**Caution**: SmolbeanPlanet3D doesn't explicitly implement this; it's a recommendation.

**Next Step**: Phase 3 enhancement; not critical for Phase 1 MVP

---

### 10. Save/Load Serialization Framework (Rewrite, Don't Reuse)

**Rank**: ⭐⭐☆☆☆ (2/5 — INCOMPATIBLE, BUT PATTERN IS USEFUL)

**Files**:
- [Assets/Scripts/Game/SaveGameManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Game/SaveGameManager.cs)
- [Assets/Scripts/Data/SaveFileData.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Data/SaveFileData.cs)

**What You Get**:
- **Design pattern**: De/serialize entire world state
- **Scope lesson**: What needs persisting (seed, build positions, creature states)
- **Format choice**: JSON (human-readable, large); alternative = binary (compact, fast)

**Effort to Adapt**:
- **LOW**: Concept only; implementation must be rewritten for ECS
- **MEDIUM**: Design binary format for your world data

**Total Adaptation Time**: 2–3 weeks (Phase 2, integrated with ECS component serialization)

**Why Included (But Low Rank)**:
- Current implementation is **incompatible with ECS** (uses object-name lookups, type metadata)
- Pattern is valuable (all games need save/load)
- But MUST be rebuilt for your architecture

**Caution**: Do NOT reuse `SaveGameManager.cs` — rewrite from scratch with binary format.

**Next Step**: Phase 2 — Design ECS-compatible serialization; use Type.GetProperties() reflection for ComponentData

---

## Implementation Roadmap Summary

| Priority | Concept | Phase | Timeline | Effort |
|----------|---------|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | WFC Solver + Schema | 1 | 2–3 weeks | Low–Med |
| 2 | Mesh Boundary Authoring | 1 | 3–5 days | Med |
| 3 | Height Field Pipeline | 1 | 1 week | Low |
| 4 | Terrain Mesh Library | 1 | 1–2 weeks | Low–Med |
| 5 | Wear + Feedback System | 2–3 | 1–2 weeks | Med–High |
| 6 | GPU Instanced Vegetation | 2–3 | 1–2 weeks | Med |
| 7 | Deterministic PRNG | 1 | 2–3 days | Low |
| 8 | Feature Placement | 1–2 | 1 week | Low–Med |
| 9 | Biome Weighting | 3 | 1–2 weeks | Med |
| 10 | Save/Load (REWRITE) | 2 | 2–3 weeks | High |

---

## What NOT to Reuse

❌ **GameStateManager singleton** — Replace with ECS systems
❌ **Per-tile GameObject instantiation** — Use chunked meshes + Entities Graphics
❌ **SaveGameManager JSON serialization** — Use binary + ComponentData
❌ **Global Random usage** — Replace with deterministic Xorshift128
❌ **BuildingController / AnimalController** — Domain-specific to SmolbeanPlanet3D

---

## Files Worth Exploring (Reference Only)

- [Assets/Scripts/Game/GridManager.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Game/GridManager.cs) — Spatial management pattern (adapt, don't copy)
- [Assets/Scripts/Creatures/SmolbeanAnimal.cs](../../Assets/Scripts/Creatures/SmolbeanAnimal.cs) — Creature base class (reference for state machine + buff system)
- [Assets/Materials/](../../Assets/Materials/) — Shader library (all portable, many directly reusable)
